website_accounts_table.notes:
Primary personal email account. Created around 2009. Used for most online accounts, financial notifications, family correspondence, and online shopping receipts.

Recovery email: sarah.thompson.family+recovery@gmail.com (spouse access)
Recovery phone: (206) 555-3821

Two-factor authentication (2FA):
- Enabled via Google Authenticator on personal iPhone
- Backup codes printed and stored in home safe in labeled envelope: "Google Backup Codes – Michael".

Estate note: This account receives statements and notices from banks, brokerage, utilities, and many online services. Executor or spouse should access this to:
- Locate bills and recurring subscriptions
- Notify contacts of my passing
- Recover/reset passwords for other accounts tied to this email.

Important: Do NOT delete this account until all financial and legal matters are fully settled, as many password reset links and account verifications go here.

Work email associated with employer: North Sound Architects, PLLC (Seattle, WA).

Used strictly for professional matters: client communication, project documents, HR/payroll notifications, health insurance and 401(k) plan access links, and professional organization membership communications.

Access: This account is controlled by employer IT; password changes and account access after my death would be handled through the company’s HR and IT departments, not by family directly.

Estate note: Spouse or executor should contact:
- HR at North Sound Architects, main line: (206) 555-9100
- Request connection to HR and IT to:
  * Notify them of my death
  * Arrange for out-of-office message
  * Obtain necessary employment records, benefits, final paycheck, 401(k) rollover options, and life insurance information linked to employment.

Family does NOT need my password; company policy prohibits sharing. This entry is here so they know which employer to contact and what email domain is used.

Old secondary email account, rarely used since around 2016. Some legacy accounts and newsletters may still be tied to this address.

Purpose:
- Historical account from early 2000s
- May still receive:
  * Legacy newsletter spam
  * Old forum notifications
  * Occasional password reset options for very old accounts I may have forgotten about

2FA: Not enabled. Security questions set long ago and may be outdated.

Estate note: This account is not critical but may be useful for:
- Locating any forgotten online services or subscriptions
- Canceling any lingering services if password reset emails still route here

If access is not possible or too difficult, it’s acceptable to let this account lapse after major estate tasks are completed.

Personal Facebook account used since around 2008.

Primary uses:
- Keeping in touch with extended family and old classmates
- Sharing family photos, holiday updates, and major life events
- Participating in neighborhood and local community groups (Seattle-area parenting group, local architecture interest group)

2FA:
- Enabled via SMS to cell: (206) 555-3821

Legacy contact:
- My wife, Sarah Thompson, is set as my Facebook Legacy Contact.
- She should have the ability (per Facebook’s policy) to:
  * Memorialize the account after my death
  * Pin a post to my profile with funeral/memorial information
  * Respond to friend requests (e.g., extended family) and update profile picture/cover photo

Estate note:
- I prefer that this account be memorialized rather than deleted.
- Sarah can also download an archive of posts and photos if she wants to preserve them for our children.
- No direct financial accounts are linked here, but some apps/websites use Facebook Login; if Sarah encounters those, she can either use this account or reset them via email.

Instagram account primarily for sharing family photos, travel pictures, and home renovation projects.

Usage:
- Linked to Facebook for cross-posting
- Mostly followed by close friends, coworkers, and relatives
- Occasionally used to message friends about meetups or events

2FA:
- Enabled via SMS to (206) 555-3821

Estate note:
- No financial data stored here.
- I would prefer this account to be either memorialized or quietly deleted after my passing, at Sarah’s discretion.
- Some photos are unique; Sarah may want to download or save them to our local family photo archive before deletion.

Login recovery:
- Connected email: michael.r.thompson74@gmail.com.

Twitter/X account used mainly for architecture news, Seattle city planning updates, and following sports (Seahawks and Mariners).

Usage:
- Occasional tweets about architecture, city zoning, and local events
- No significant personal or family photos
- No direct financial accounts linked, other than potentially some app authorizations

2FA:
- App-based 2FA via Google Authenticator on my iPhone

Estate note:
- Not essential to maintain.
- Fine to deactivate or delete once any active app connections are verified as non-critical.
- Might contain some bookmarked links to professional articles, but nothing critical to the estate.

Professional LinkedIn profile: Michael R. Thompson, Architect, based in Seattle, WA.

Usage:
- Maintains professional work history and project portfolio
- Connected to coworkers, former colleagues, and some clients
- Used to receive job-related messages, recruiter inquiries, and industry news

Estate note:
- After my death, Sarah or the executor may wish to:
  * Post a brief notice for professional contacts, or
  * Ask a trusted coworker (e.g., David Chen at North Sound Architects) to share a memorial note on LinkedIn.
- Eventually, this account can be memorialized or deactivated per LinkedIn’s process.

No direct financial accounts linked; however, employer benefits information or contact details for HR may be accessible indirectly through connections.

website_accounts_table.password_use_a_strong_one: Same as primary Gmail (see Gmail entry)
website_accounts_table.notes:
Central repository for most of our family’s digital photos and videos since approximately 2012.

Content:
- Family vacations (Oregon Coast, Yellowstone 2018, San Diego 2021)
- Children’s birthdays, school events, sports, and holidays
- Home renovation progress photos
- Scans of older printed family photos (uploaded around 2019)

Access & Sharing:
- Linked to main Google account (Gmail login).
- Shared albums with Sarah’s Google account: sarah.elizabeth.thompson@gmail.com.
- Some albums shared with grandparents and siblings.

Estate note:
- This is one of the most important digital assets for sentimental reasons.
- Sarah should have full access through shared libraries and via my Google account (see backup codes in safe).
- Recommend downloading a full archive via Google Takeout and storing:
  * On external hard drive in home safe
  * In a secondary cloud backup under Sarah’s account.

Do NOT delete this account or its contents until Sarah has confirmed that everything is safely backed up in multiple locations.

Apple ID used for:
- iPhone and iPad backups
- iCloud Drive documents
- iCloud Photos (some overlap with Google Photos; more recent phone photos are here)
- App Store purchases and subscriptions

Devices linked:
- Personal iPhone 13 (main device)
- iPad (shared with kids for schoolwork)

2FA:
- Required by Apple; code sent to trusted devices (iPhone and iPad).

Estate note:
- Important for accessing:
  * Recent photos and videos not yet copied to Google Photos
  * Any notes stored in Apple Notes (including a few home maintenance records and measurements)
  * App subscriptions (e.g., music, storage plans) that may need cancellation.

Instructions:
- Sarah knows the device passcode for my phone.
- From the phone, she can reset this password if needed.
- Before closing or changing anything, ensure:
  * Photos are backed up to an external drive and/or Google Photos
  * Subscription list is reviewed (Settings > Apple ID > Subscriptions) and anything unnecessary is canceled.

Apple may require a death certificate and proof of relationship to transfer or access this account long-term.

Amazon account used for:
- Household purchases (groceries, household supplies, kids’ school supplies)
- Occasional electronics and books
- Kindle e-books

Prime:
- Amazon Prime membership active, billed annually to joint credit card ending in 4421.

Associated addresses:
- Home: 4821 NE 61st Street, Seattle, WA 98115

Estate note:
- Important to review:
  * Active subscriptions (e.g., Subscribe & Save items like coffee, paper towels)
  * Any digital content (Kindle books) that may be shared via family library

Actions:
- Sarah can continue using this account if she wishes; it’s a household account.
- If desired, she can update payment methods and email contact to her own.
- Ensure any recurring deliveries are still wanted; cancel those that are not.

Household Netflix streaming account.

Usage:
- Shared with immediate family only (Sarah and kids).
- Profiles: "Michael", "Sarah", "Kids".

Billing:
- Charged monthly to joint credit card ending in 4421.

Estate note:
- Sarah can continue using this account normally.
- If credit cards are reissued or changed, ensure Netflix billing is updated to avoid service interruption.

No special estate actions required beyond ensuring billing continuity or canceling if no longer needed.

Spotify Premium Family plan.

Usage:
- Family members on plan: Michael, Sarah, and two kids.
- Used daily for music at home and in the car.

Billing:
- Monthly fee on joint credit card ending in 4421.

Estate note:
- Not essential for estate administration.
- Sarah can either:
  * Take over the account and update payment details, or
  * Cancel if she prefers a different music service.

No significant personal data beyond listening history and playlists.

Cloud storage mainly for:
- Work-related reference documents (non-confidential)
- Personal spreadsheets (household budget, vacation planning)
- Scanned copies of certain household documents (appliance manuals, car maintenance records)

Note:
- LEGAL and FINANCIAL documents relevant to the estate (wills, life insurance, etc.) are primarily stored on a separate encrypted external drive and in paper form, not only in Dropbox. Some duplicates may be here as a convenience.

Estate note:
- Check for any:
  * Recent versions of household budget spreadsheets
  * Lists of account numbers (some redacted) that may help cross-reference.
- After confirming contents are backed up elsewhere, this account can be left alone or closed as desired.

Linked email: Gmail primary account.

Note-taking account used heavily between 2013–2020, less frequently afterwards.

Contents:
- Home maintenance logs (e.g., roof replacement 2017, HVAC service dates)
- Car maintenance notes (oil change intervals, tire replacements)
- Travel planning notes and checklists
- Some clipped web articles on investing and personal finance

Estate note:
- May contain references to account names and institutions (e.g., "Vanguard taxable account", "Fidelity 401(k)") but generally NOT full account numbers or passwords.
- Useful as a cross-check to ensure all financial institutions have been contacted.
- Can be exported or printed if needed; otherwise, can be left dormant or closed after estate is settled.

Primary investment and retirement account provider.

Accounts held:
- Rollover IRA (from previous employer)
- Joint taxable brokerage account with Sarah

2FA:
- SMS-based 2FA to (206) 555-3821 and security questions.

Estate note:
- This is a key financial institution for the estate.
- Account numbers and beneficiary designations are documented separately in the estate binder stored in the home safe.

Instructions:
- Executor or Sarah should contact Vanguard directly by phone (number on statements) with:
  * Death certificate
  * Any required estate documents
- Online access can be reset by Vanguard; password here is for reference and may be obsolete.

Do not rely solely on this login; follow formal procedures with Vanguard’s estate department.

Employer-sponsored 401(k) plan managed by Fidelity NetBenefits.

Usage:
- Salary deferrals and employer match
- Investment in target-date and index funds

Access:
- Login sometimes initiated from employer HR portal, then redirects to Fidelity.

Estate note:
- This retirement account lists Sarah as primary beneficiary (confirmed 2023).
- To claim benefits, Sarah should:
  * Contact Fidelity’s beneficiary services line (number on 401(k) statements)
  * Provide death certificate and any forms required
  * Coordinate with employer HR, as they may need to confirm employment status and plan details.

Online login information may change; formal claim process is more important than password access.

Household banking with JPMorgan Chase.

Accounts accessed:
- Joint checking account (primary household expenses)
- Joint savings account (emergency fund)
- Joint credit card ending in 4421 (used for most recurring online subscriptions)

2FA:
- SMS verification to both my cell and Sarah’s cell.

Estate note:
- As joint owner, Sarah retains full rights of survivorship and can access funds without needing this password.
- However, this login is useful for:
  * Reviewing transaction history
  * Identifying recurring online charges and subscriptions
  * Downloading statements for estate records

Formal banking actions (e.g., retitling accounts, closing credit cards) should be done directly with Chase by visiting a branch or calling the customer service number on the back of the card.

Online account for natural gas and electric utility service at home address.

Usage:
- View and pay monthly utility bills
- Track usage and set up budget billing (if enabled)

Billing:
- Auto-pay set up via joint checking account.

Estate note:
- If Sarah keeps living in the house, this account can remain as-is, with her taking over responsibility for monitoring.
- If the house is sold, the executor or Sarah will need to:
  * Contact PSE directly to close or transfer service
  * Ensure auto-pay is canceled at the appropriate time.

Login is convenient but not strictly necessary; phone contact with account number from a paper bill is sufficient.

Home internet and cable TV account.

Services:
- High-speed internet
- Basic cable package

Billing:
- Auto-pay on joint credit card ending in 4421.

Estate note:
- If Sarah and kids remain in the house, service likely continues unchanged.
- If the home is sold or service is changed, the account will need to be:
  * Canceled or
  * Transferred to Sarah’s name.

Use this login to adjust service levels, review equipment returns, and confirm billing status.

Costco membership associated with household shopping.

Usage:
- Online ordering (bulk household goods)
- Warehouse membership card under Michael as primary, Sarah as additional cardholder.

Estate note:
- After my death, Sarah can:
  * Visit membership desk to convert herself to primary member
  * Update payment methods

Online login is helpful for reviewing online orders and managing membership renewals.

website_accounts_table.logon_or_user_name: Library card number ending 3827 (check physical card)
website_accounts_table.notes:
Library account used for:
- E-books and audiobooks via Libby/OverDrive
- Occasional physical book holds

Estate note:
- Not critical for estate administration.
- Fines, if any, are usually small and not a major financial concern.
- Account can simply go dormant or be closed by contacting the library if desired.

Included here mainly so family understands why library-related emails may appear in Gmail inbox.

website_accounts_table.password_use_a_strong_one: Same as primary Gmail (see Gmail entry)
website_accounts_table.notes:
Cloud storage within Google account.

Contents:
- Household budget spreadsheets
- Simple net worth snapshots updated annually
- Kids’ school documents (scanned report cards, enrollment forms)
- Some scanned receipts for large purchases (appliances, major home repairs)

Estate note:
- Check the "Finances" and "Home" folders for:
  * Helpful summaries of accounts and approximate balances
  * Notes on home improvement work and warranties

These documents complement, but do not replace, the formal estate binder kept in the home safe.

Online payment account linked to:
- Joint checking account
- Joint credit card ending in 4421

Usage:
- Occasional online purchases from smaller merchants
- Rarely used to send money to friends or family

Estate note:
- Check recent activity for any:
  * Recurring payments or subscriptions
  * Outstanding balances or refunds

If not needed, executor or Sarah can close the account after confirming that all linked subscriptions are handled.

Peer-to-peer payment app.

Usage:
- Splitting restaurant bills with friends
- Paying other parents for kids’ activity fees
- Occasional reimbursements for shared expenses

Linked to:
- Joint checking account

Estate note:
- Check Venmo balance; transfer any remaining funds to joint checking.
- Review recent activity to ensure no unresolved payment requests.
- Account can be closed once balance is zero and no pending transactions exist.

Free/basic Zoom account.

Usage:
- Occasional video calls with extended family
- Rare work-related calls when not using employer’s account

Estate note:
- Not important for estate administration.
- Can be ignored or left inactive.

Neighborhood social network.

Usage:
- Stay informed about local events, lost pets, crime alerts, and community recommendations.

Estate note:
- No financial data.
- After my death, Sarah can continue using it or let the account go dormant.
- Might be a convenient place to share estate sale or moving information if needed.

Online discussion account.

Subreddits:
- r/architecture
- r/personalfinance
- r/Seattle

Estate note:
- No real-name information prominently displayed; mostly anonymous participation.
- No financial accounts linked.
- This account can be ignored or left as-is; no estate action required.

website_accounts_table.password_use_a_strong_one: Same as primary Gmail (see Gmail entry)
website_accounts_table.notes:
YouTube account tied to Google.

Usage:
- Subscribed to home repair, architecture, and travel channels
- A few personal family videos set to "unlisted" for sharing with relatives

Estate note:
- Sarah may want to check for any unlisted family videos and download them for safekeeping.
- No financial accounts directly tied here beyond Google account settings.

Credit monitoring service.

Usage:
- Periodically checking credit score and credit report
- Monitoring for new accounts or suspicious activity

Estate note:
- Could be useful for:
  * Checking for any overlooked credit accounts
  * Monitoring identity theft risks shortly after my death

After estate is settled and all accounts are closed or transferred as needed, this service can be canceled or left inactive.

Used for annual income tax filing.

Contents:
- Copies of past federal and state tax returns
- Records of W-2s, 1099s, and other tax documents

Estate note:
- Executor or Sarah should review prior returns to:
  * Confirm list of financial institutions (banks, brokerages)
  * See history of deductions and possible carryovers

Important: Access may require two-factor authentication via email or SMS.

Even if online access is difficult, paper copies of the most recent returns are stored in the home filing cabinet labeled "Taxes" and in the estate binder.

Electronic health record portal for UW Medicine.

Usage:
- View lab results, visit summaries, and messages from primary care physician
- Manage appointments and prescription refills

Estate note:
- Might be useful to review:
  * Recent diagnoses
  * Medication list
  * Physician contact info

However, for formal medical records and death certificate details, healthcare providers will work directly with next of kin and not rely solely on this portal.

Account can be left inactive after estate matters are complete.
